title: lyciodes crenulatum
intro: Lyciodes crenulatum, also known as the Creeping Thyme or Wild Thyme, is a low-growing, flowering plant that belongs to the Lamiaceae family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory properties, making it effective in treating wounds, reducing fever, and alleviating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The health benefits of Lyciodes crenulatum include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce stress, and improve digestion, making it a popular herbal remedy in many cultures. In terms of herbal preparations, Lyciodes crenulatum can be consumed as a tea, used in topical creams and ointments, or added to soups and salads for its culinary and medicinal properties.
